records.ety: [Freq. of OE. straken to roam, to stroke. See Stroke, v. t.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Straggled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Straggling (?).]

defs: To wander from the direct course or way; to rove; to stray; to wander from the line of march or desert the line of battle; as, when troops are on the march, the men should not straggle.
To wander at large; to roam idly about; to ramble.
quotes: The wolf spied out a straggling kid.

defs: To escape or stretch beyond proper limits, as the branches of a plant; to spread widely apart; to shoot too far or widely in growth.
quotes: Trim off the small, superfluous branches on each side of the hedge that straggle too far out.

defs: To be dispersed or separated; to occur at intervals.
quotes: They came between Scylla and Charybdis and the straggling rocks.
